classes.reasoning: The document is titled 'eCRF — COGNITIVE ASSESSMENT MODULE' at the top and explicitly labeled 'Form NEU-CRF-004 v1.1'. It contains structured data-capture fields for patient assessments including Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR), Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE), and falls assessment, with checkboxes, scoring tables, and signature blocks typical of case report forms.

summary: This is an electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) designed to capture cognitive assessment data for individual patients at multiple scheduled visits (Baseline through Week 78 and End of Treatment). It includes standardized neurological instruments: the Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR), Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE), and a falls assessment with Morse Fall Scale scoring, all to be completed by a trained assessor before study drug dispensing.
content:
eCRF — COGNITIVE ASSESSMENT MODULE


Study BIORCE-NEU-2022-004 | Form NEU-CRF-004 v1.1




Module v1.1 | Medidata Rave






Patient Code
VEL209-___-____


Site ID
______


Visit




-- Select --


Baseline (Week 0)


Week 13
Week 26


Week 52
Week 78
EOT






Date
__ / __ / ______




⚠ Complete all assessments BEFORE study drug dispensing. Quiet, private room. Study partner waits outside.


A. CDR — CLINICAL DEMENTIA RATING




Domain
0 — Normal
0.5 — Questionable
1 — Mild
2 — Moderate
3 — Severe


Memory


Orientation


Judgement & Problem Solving


Community Affairs


Home & Hobbies


Personal Care


CDR-SB (sum all 6 domains, 0–18)




B. MMSE




Domain
Max
Score


Orientation to time
5


Orientation to place
5


Registration (3 words)
3


Attention & Calculation
5


Recall (3 words)
3


Language (naming, repeat, command, read, write)
8


Visuospatial (copy pentagons)
1


MMSE TOTAL
30




C. FALLS ASSESSMENT


Falls since last visit: [ ] None   [ ] 1   [ ] 2   [ ] 3+

Any fall with injury? [ ] Yes — document as AE   [ ] No

Morse Fall Scale Total: _______   Risk: [ ] Low (0-24)   [ ] Medium (25-44)   [ ] High (>=45)




Assessor Name (PRINT)


Assessor Signature


Date (DD/MM/YYYY)
